title: Session Completion File
description: Canonical machine-readable final result of one session (§11.3). Usually written by noted at the end of post-processing; briefing watch may also write a failed startup completion when a planned launch never starts before the event window closes. briefing reads this file first when deciding how to proceed; it must never be inferred from file presence or log parsing (guardrail 3).

schema_version.description: Major.minor version of this completion payload. Readers pinned to major 1 accept any 1.x payload; forward-tolerate minor (unknown fields ignored). Enforced as a major-1 pattern rather than a const so a 1.0 reader does not reject a 1.1 payload at schema-validation time (see `contracts/versioning-policy.md`).

manifest_schema_version.description: schema_version of the manifest that produced this session. Present for traceability as schemas evolve.

terminal_status.description: Final session state. Locked vocabulary per §26.2.

stop_reason.description: Why the session ended. Locked vocabulary per §26.1. See §11.5 for the stop_reason → terminal_status mapping.

audio_capture_ok.description: True if at least one usable raw-audio file was flushed to audio/.

transcript_ok.description: True if transcript.txt and transcript.json were produced.

diarization_ok.description: True if diarization produced usable labels. May be false with transcript_ok=true; summarisation falls back to speaker-agnostic style (§19.2).

errors.description: Fatal issues that led to a terminal_status of failed or contributed to completed_with_warnings.

completed_at.description: Time post-processing finished. ISO-8601 with explicit timezone offset.
